text_blocks.text: Oxygen is more soluble in water than nitrogen is.
Water in equilibrium with air contains approximately
1 molecule of dissolved 0 2 for every 2 molecules of
N 2, compared to an atmospheric ratio of
approximately 1:4. The solubility of oxygen in water
is temperature-dependent, and about twice as much
(14.6 mg^L-1) dissolves at 0 degC than at 20 degC
atmosphere (101.3 kPa) of air, freshwater contains
about 6.04 milliliters (mL) of oxygen per liter,
whereas seawater contains about 4.95 mL per liter.
At 5 degC the solubility increases to 9.0 mL (50%
more than at 25 degC) per liter for water and 7.2
mL (45% more) per liter for sea water.
